spec_match.rationale: Demo exercises the core API surface — every title keyword (algorithm, ancestor, details, found) and 6 summary keywords appear directly in the page.

interactive.rationale: There is a try-it button and some script logic, but the interactive surface is minimal (3 form controls) — adding a live readout or more parameters would push it past the bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and concept lede share 7 keywords; the page delivers what the index promises.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered; the page does not visibly handle missing-API, empty-input, or error branches — those edges are the spec's harder territory.

references.rationale: Page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5179013869993984 directly in the see-also / references section.

design_system.rationale: All colours flow through CSS variables, no raw hex, and button overrides keep colour and background in sync.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: The page has the bones of an interactive demo but only 9 controls. Add another axis the user can vary (a second parameter, a different input mode, or an explicit before/after toggle) and route the result into a visible readout.

openQuestions.title: Add an explicit edge-case branch
openQuestions.detail: The page covers the happy path. Pick at least one failure mode from the spec (missing permission, empty input, unsupported value, rate-limit, etc.) and wire a button that drives the API into that branch with the resulting error / fallback shown in the page.

summary: Hits the bar but several criteria sit at partial — the open questions list concrete additions that would close the gap.
